title: salix glauca var. subincurva
intro: Salix glauca var. subincurva, also known as the bent willow or crooked willow, is a variety of Salix glauca, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of the Northern Hemisphere. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Salix glauca var. subincurva include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as treating conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making teas, tinctures, or infusions from its bark, leaves, or roots, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
